rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Implementation-level AGREE bindings (assign/assert) must reference valid subcomponents and connections present in the target implementation scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE fusion by placing annex content in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Use only identifiers and references visible in the current AADL component scope when generating AGREE annex content.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts define interface behavior; implementation contracts define subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or features not visible in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
